events.title: Israel Ramps Up Southern Lebanon Attacks Ahead of US Talks
events.desc: Israel intensified its attacks in southern Lebanon, resulting in two fatalities. This escalation occurred just before crucial talks scheduled in Washington aimed at extending a ceasefire. The ongoing conflict between Israel and Hezbollah continues despite previous U.S.-backed truce efforts.

events.title: Russia Launches Hundreds More Drones at Ukraine
events.desc: Russia conducted a significant drone assault on Ukraine, firing over 1,560 drones since Wednesday. The attacks resulted in three civilian deaths, highlighting the persistent aerial threat to Ukrainian infrastructure and population centers.

events.title: Iran Allowing Transit of Chinese Vessels in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: Iran has reportedly begun permitting some Chinese vessels to transit the Strait of Hormuz following an understanding on management protocols. This development occurred as U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping agreed on the necessity of open passage through the strait for energy flow during Trump's visit to Beijing.

events.title: Iran and UAE Clash at BRICS Foreign Ministers' Meeting
events.desc: Iran's foreign minister accused the United Arab Emirates of direct involvement in military operations against Iran during a BRICS meeting in New Delhi. This diplomatic spat follows the UAE's denial of a statement by Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu regarding his visit to the Gulf country during the Iran war.

events.title: Lebanon to Press Israel for Ceasefire at Washington Talks
events.desc: A senior Lebanese official stated that Lebanon will demand an immediate ceasefire from Israel during face-to-face talks in Washington. Hostilities between Hezbollah and Israel persist despite a U.S.-backed truce announced last month. The talks are proceeding despite objections from Hezbollah.

events.title: Satellite Imagery Shows Ongoing Demolitions in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Satellite imagery reveals extensive damage in southern Lebanon, with at least 46 out of 54 towns and villages within the Israeli Defense Forces' 'Yellow Line' heavily damaged or flattened. Much of this destruction has occurred despite a fragile ceasefire agreed upon last month.

events.title: Israeli Strikes in Southern Lebanon Kill 22 People
events.desc: A series of Israeli air strikes in southern Lebanon resulted in the deaths of 22 people, including eight children. The attacks targeted the southern region and Beirut, underscoring the ongoing violence despite ceasefire efforts.

events.title: Lebanon Says Israeli Strikes Killed 39
events.desc: Lebanon reported that 39 people were killed in Israeli strikes, including two paramedics who were on a rescue mission. The conflict between Israel and Hezbollah has continued despite a ceasefire agreement announced last month.

events.title: Trump Calls Iran Ceasefire 'On Massive Life Support'
events.desc: U.S. President Donald Trump described the current ceasefire in the Iran war as being 'on massive life support,' criticizing an Iranian counteroffer to end the conflict as 'unbelievably weak.' Peace talks have stalled, and the war's economic costs continue to rise.

events.title: Trump Calls Iran Response 'Totally Unacceptable'
events.desc: President Donald Trump deemed Iran's response to a U.S. proposal to end the war 'totally unacceptable.' Reports indicate Iran is seeking the lifting of the U.S. naval blockade, recognition of its sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and compensation for war damages.

events.title: Iran War Overshadows Trump's China Visit as Peace Talks Stall
events.desc: The ongoing Iran war is a significant backdrop to President Donald Trump's visit to China, where he is expected to seek Beijing's assistance in ending the conflict. Peace talks have stalled, and the global economic impact of the war is increasing.

events.title: Iran Grants Internet Access to Select Few After Months of Blackout
events.desc: Following months of a near-total internet blackout during the Middle East war, Iran has begun providing internet access to a select group of users through a privileged service. This move has generated criticism amid millions remaining offline since the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran.

events.title: South Korea Official: Iran Likely Behind Hormuz Ship Attack
events.desc: A senior South Korean official indicated that the likelihood of an entity other than Iran being responsible for the attack on a South Korean cargo vessel near the Strait of Hormuz is low. South Korea is analyzing intelligence shared by the U.S. regarding the May 4 attack.

events.title: Hezbollah Support Endures in South Lebanon Despite Ceasefire Failures
events.desc: Despite ongoing Israeli attacks and occupation, many residents in southern Lebanon continue to support Hezbollah, viewing it as the only force capable of defense. The ceasefire has failed to halt the conflict between Hezbollah and Israel.

events.title: Israel Passes Law Allowing Death Penalty and Public Trials for 7 October Attack Perpetrators
events.desc: Israel has enacted a law that permits the death penalty and public trials for individuals linked to the Hamas-led attacks on October 7. This legislative action follows the unprecedented attacks and aims to implement stricter legal measures.
